title: Make canonical next_action the sole continuation dispatcher
goal: Update the runtime so valid canonical next_action is the only authoritative continuation dispatcher, while status remains a lifecycle classification field and optional metadata remains non-authoritative.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 protocol depends on deterministic continuation. That requires eliminating status-derived dispatch heuristics and ensuring continuation is driven by the minimal canonical continuation contract, especially top-level next_action.
scope: Review all runtime code paths that choose what action happens next for an active transaction.
Identify branches that dispatch continuation from status, checkpoint substate, helper labels, phase-style metadata, step-style metadata, or other non-canonical signals.
Refactor continuation logic so valid canonical next_action is the single authoritative dispatcher.
Preserve status as lifecycle classification for validation, reporting, and diagnostics only.
Ensure no-active baseline handling still dispatches through next_action: tx.begin without special-case heuristic reconstruction.
Ensure resumed verified, committed, and repair-loop states continue according to canonical next_action rather than inferred status flow.
out_of_scope: Broad compatibility normalization beyond what is required to stop non-canonical dispatch behavior.
Adding full regression coverage for all interruption cases.
expected_outputs: A list of continuation entrypoints and dispatcher branches in the runtime.
A code update that routes continuation through canonical next_action whenever it is present and valid.
A short implementation note explaining where status remains authoritative and where it no longer controls dispatch.
A list of any legacy or fallback branches that remain and why they are still safe under the 0.6.0 protocol.
continuation_rules_to_enforce: When valid canonical next_action is present, it must be preferred over status-derived heuristics.
Status must remain a lifecycle classification field, not a dispatcher.
Checkpoint substates such as verify_state and commit_state must not silently replace top-level continuation control.
Phase-style, step-style, session-derived, or guidance-only fields must not be required for continuation correctness.
Planning artifacts must not participate in continuation dispatch.
review_targets: Any function that resumes an active transaction.
Any function that chooses follow-up actions after verify or commit.
Any function that branches directly on status to decide the next runtime step.
Any helper or orchestration layer that reconstructs continuation instead of consuming canonical next_action.
Any no-active baseline branch that starts new work without going through the canonical continuation contract.
Commit-side helper and orchestration code that derives continuation from phase, current_step, nested status, or session context instead of canonical top-level next_action.
acceptance_criteria: Every continuation entrypoint in the runtime has been reviewed for next_action versus status dispatch behavior, including repo_tools.py and commit_manager.py helper flows.
The runtime does not use status as the primary continuation dispatcher when valid canonical next_action is available.
The runtime does not require optional phase-style or step-style metadata to determine the next action.
The runtime does not require session-derived metadata to determine deterministic continuation when canonical next_action is valid.
No-active baseline continuation remains structurally represented and proceeds through canonical next_action: tx.begin.
Verified, committed, and repair-loop resumes follow canonical next_action rather than inferred status flow.
The resulting behavior matches the plan: status classifies, next_action dispatches.
The ticket must not be marked in-progress while dependency p1-t02 remains non-terminal in the ticket index.
implementation_notes: Prefer deleting or bypassing status-driven dispatch heuristics rather than adding more metadata to reconcile them.
If fallback handling is unavoidable, it must be clearly bounded and must not override a valid canonical next_action.
Any retained fallback branch must be documented as non-primary behavior and justified against the 0.6.0 plan.
notes: This ticket is high leverage because ambiguous dispatcher logic would leak into verify, commit, terminal, and resume behavior.
All code review and implementation decisions must be checked against the minimal canonical continuation contract defined in the plan.

Current review finding: dependency p1-t02 is still non-terminal in docs/v0.6.0/tickets_list.json, so this ticket cannot honestly remain in-progress until that dependency reality changes or the dependency list is revised.

Current review finding: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_load_tx_context still derives helper execution context from active_tx.phase and active_tx.current_step, so continuation is not yet driven solely by canonical top-level next_action.

Current review finding: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::repo_verify still checks terminal eligibility from nested active_tx.status rather than treating top-level canonical status and next_action as the authoritative continuation contract.

Current review finding: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_emit_tx_event still writes phase/current_step/session_id back into materialized active_tx before state synchronization, which preserves non-canonical dispatcher inputs in helper flows.

Current review finding: src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py::_load_tx_context still treats session_id as required helper execution context and therefore exceeds the fixed plan's secondary-session role.

Current review finding: src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py::_emit_tx_event still writes phase/current_step/session_id and nested status-driven verify/commit transitions back into materialized active_tx, so commit-side continuation is not yet driven solely by canonical top-level next_action.

Current review finding: tests/test_repo_tools.py still encodes old-shaped active_tx fixtures and expectations around phase/current_step/session_id-driven continuation, so p2-t05 needs paired runtime and regression updates.

Current review finding: tests/test_commit_manager.py also needs review and likely updates because it currently validates commit-side continuation through helper-mutated phase/current_step/status state rather than the minimal canonical continuation contract.
